prompt:
Make an demo video of our new AI Workflow builder feature. Show Text “Introducing The AI Workflow Builder”

Make it two scenes: one text, and one with screenshot.

--- TEMPLATE STEERING ---
Maintain professional motion-design quality and clear visual hierarchy.
Keep text readable at all times with safe contrast and spacing.
Avoid clutter; use deliberate pacing and polished transitions.
Template mode: ai-workflow-builder.
Default template baseline: prioritize graphics-design framing over website/dashboard layout, keep the hero block dominant (roughly 40-75% width), and keep headline typography oversized (roughly 8-16% frame height).
Use a deterministic two-scene workflow-builder narrative with strong text hierarchy.
Scene 1 should read as bold kinetic typography, not dashboard chrome.
Scene 2 should focus on UI interaction storytelling with subtle movement and clear focal targets.
Keep animations precise, render-safe, and free of noisy decorative effects.
Large typography is mandatory: hero headline should be roughly 10-16% of frame height with strong width occupancy; avoid small text in primary beats.
Keep supporting copy secondary; primary beats must be driven by oversized title lockups rather than dense paragraph text.
Favor deterministic, render-safe scene composition over excessive complexity.
Show this exact hero text as a dominant readable lockup: Introducing The AI Workflow Builder.
Scene 1 must animate zoom-out text beats in exact order: Hiring sucks, Managing sucks, Scaling sucks -> Scaling sucked.
In Scene 1, the '(ed)' emphasis on 'sucked' must be visibly circled as a graphic annotation while keeping text readability high.
Scene 2 must transpile the attached image component, preserve its key text-field/send-button semantics, then animate a floating cursor moving toward the send button.
If a screenshot seed or component reference is present, treat it as the authoritative workflow-builder showcase source in Scene 2.
Scene 2 camera should add a slight incremental zoom-in over time while cursor motion stays smooth and deterministic.

--- SCENERY DIRECTIVES ---
Global scenery mood: soft, neutral.
Motion profile: calm.
Scene 1 @0f for 90f: clean gradient backdrop, camera none, transition fade.

--- HARDCODED IMPLEMENTATION ---
Implementation id: ai-workflow-builder
This request uses the AI Workflow Builder hardcoded flow.
Output exactly 2 scenes.
Use exact on-screen text: Introducing The AI Workflow Builder.
Scene 1 must animate zoom-out text beats in order: Hiring sucks, Managing sucks, Scaling sucks -> Scaling sucked, and emphasize a circled (ed).
Scene 2 must feature the AI Workflow Builder interface narrative and keep motion deterministic/render-safe.
Scene 2 must transpile the attached image component, treat the provided screenshot/component reference as authoritative workflow-builder UI source, then animate a floating cursor toward the send button with a slight incremental zoom.

--- OUTPUT CONTRACT ---
Return strict JSON only (no markdown, no prose) in this exact shape:
{ "scene_envelope": { "mode": "replace_timeline|append_scene|edit_selected_scene", "scenes": [ { "id": string, "name": string, "code": string?, "base_code_ref": string?, "code_extensions": { "imports": string?, "setup": string?, "jsx": string? }?, "duration_in_frames": number, "created_at": number, "updated_at": number } ], "selected_scene_id": string?, "summary": string? }, "transition_program": { "version": "1", "quality_tier": "cinematic|dynamic|minimal", "handoffs": [ { "from_scene_id": string, "to_scene_id": string, "duration_in_frames": number, "blend_mode": "normal|screen|overlay|multiply|soft-light|hard-light", "anchor_policy": "preserve-subject|center-weighted|edge-biased", "intensity_profile": "ease-in|ease-out|ease-in-out|pulse", "layers": [ { "stage": "pre-warp|frequency|color|light|matte|blend", "preset": string, "intensity": number, "params": object? } ] } ] } }
Use mode: replace_timeline.
Each scene must remain production-safe TSX with imports and a default export.
No baseline scaffold is available: emit full `scene.code` for each scene.
Do not reference browser globals or DOM APIs; keep all logic render-safe and deterministic.
Motion language is mandatory: include transition_program handoffs for every adjacent scene pair.
For animation generation, produce exactly 2 scenes per timeline.
Each scene should target 2-3 seconds (60-90 frames at 30fps).
Transition durations must stay within 12-24 frames at 30fps for snappy pacing.
Favor active camera/layer movement, bold readable hierarchy, and deterministic render performance.
Avoid heavy render patterns such as huge blur radii, large repeated layer arrays, or excessive shadow stacks.
The opening beat must reveal a clear focal element within the first 6 frames.
Keep the final ~20 frames stable with minimal motion around the closing lockup.
summary: Generated a 2-scene timeline through two-phase orchestration.
